checks.finding: The design records the user-chosen build-time native-module approach, PS5.1 XOR constraints, full-over-lite precedence, and the exact bootstrap versus normal-update authority split with rationale.
Open Questions is None; all security-sensitive choices are fixed in the requirements or the user decisions and do not block implementation.
The stated assumptions are bounded existing-repository facts, including protected regression coverage and committed generated artifacts, with CI verification paths.

The design preserves the reviewed requirements: it keeps legitimate read-only forms narrow, uses PS5.1-compatible full XOR comparison, forces full on risk, and forbids runtime canonical parsing. Valid non-ASCII UTF-8 remains eligible unless it contains an ASCII trigger.
No numeric performance target applies; deterministic build-time generation and a fixed 32-byte comparator bound the added work while guard runtime avoids JSON parsing.
Deployment/CI specifies generator check before guard suites, stale-output failure, human-copy installation, and no service migration or feature flag.
The Data Plan gives a concrete staged-to-human-copy migration for protected source, canonical data, generated outputs, and the immutable runner.
The only external execution surface is existing GitHub Actions CI; its test.yml trust-anchor protection and generator check are explicitly covered. No network API is introduced.
Every component traces to REQ-001 through REQ-005 and the five scoped issues; no unrelated workflow surface is added.

Each high-risk path has a named deterministic test: guard corpus, static PS5.1 structural oracle, golden PowerShell output, risk checker parity, generated diff, loader shadow, and all-or-nothing human copy.
SKIP: no domain model is applicable to local CLI and CI enforcement logic.
